steps.title: Follow moisture into oil
steps.content: The passage gives a causal chain for frying: water evaporates from the crust, voids are left behind, and oil can fill those voids. If a section loses less moisture during frying, it should create fewer voids for oil to occupy.

steps.title: Infer lower fat content
steps.content: Fewer voids means less oil absorption, so the section that loses less moisture should end with lower fat content than a section that loses more moisture. A adds crust thickness, C adds how quickly permeability changes, and D adds final temperature, none of which follows from the mechanism. The answer is B.
